steps.title: Challenge passive displacement
steps.content: The first sentence says barnacle movement had been explained as passive displacement by water currents. The next sentence reports movement against the prevailing water flow, which supports self-initiated locomotion instead.

steps.title: Choose the weakening transition
steps.content: Choice A says the finding is contrary to the phenomenon, but the movement itself still occurs. Choice C says the finding draws a similar conclusion, when it reaches the opposite explanation. Choice D says it confirms the passive hypothesis, which is reversed. The transition should show that the new result weakens the old explanation. The answer is B.
